mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction permits and revolving credit balances indicate expanding household balance sheets and consumer spending momentum, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-signal months are not statistically higher than low-signal months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tight labor market conditions that boost wage growth and consumer confidence, leading to positive equity returns over the subsequent week as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal directional accuracy drops below 52 percent over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and consumer price inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks compressing corporate margins, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as profit growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: The interaction signal predicts negative returns in more than 65 percent of triggered periods, or forward returns show no significant difference between high-divergence and low-divergence regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 0.5 percent during a tightening cycle increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5 percent, or the signal information coefficient fails to exceed 0.02 over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth reflects broad-based economic expansion that directly lifts corporate revenue trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week as analyst upgrades cascade.
failure_condition: The sign of GDPC1 growth changes does not predict the sign of 5-day forward returns with accuracy exceeding 55 percent, or Rank IC stays below 0.01 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls increasing by more than 200,000 over a three-month moving average confirms robust employment creation that sustains consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings momentum strengthens.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of months where the 3-month payroll average exceeds 200k, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized sectoral recovery, which amplifies business investment and consumer spending cycles, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns in less than 45 percent of triggered instances, or forward returns show no significant premium over the market benchmark when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 220 combined with rising short-term policy rates signal entrenched inflationary pressure that forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, or the signal fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year walk-forward test.
